name: Archive the database with Notion and Google Drive

parameters.text: =✅ Archive the database with Notion and Google Drive — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Download from Google Drive

### Download from Google Drive

Downloads the file's binary content from Google Drive so the next node can parse it.

Ports: ①→ Parse CSV Contents · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Parse CSV Contents

Parses the incoming CSV binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming payload records to a stable schema

Next: Rows Pass Validation?

### Rows Pass Validation?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.itemId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Load Rows into Notion · ②→ Quarantine Bad Rows

### Quarantine Bad Rows

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Load Rows into Notion

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Repack as XLSX · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Repack as XLSX

Serializes items to a XLSX binary file.

Next: Archive Processed File (Google Drive)

### Archive Processed File (Google Drive)

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Align Field Names · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Telegram

### Escalate Failure to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

# Archive the database with Notion and Google Drive

**Pattern:** Backup & Archive · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Archive the database with Notion and Google Drive. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual data transforms is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Download from Google Drive → Parse CSV Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Rows Pass Validation? → Load Rows into Notion → Repack as XLSX → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to backup & archive without manual effort
- Anyone automating data transforms in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your data transforms process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
